case_title: TRACY EN YU VS. PETER FROBENIUS GIORDANO ET AL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ADRIANNA MARINI AND AUDREY POPPEL'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT. ADRIANA MARINI AND AUDREY POPPEL'S "MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT" IS DENIED. THE CASE INVOLVES A THREE-VEHICLE IN-LINE ACCIDENT ON US 101, THE FACTS OF WHICH ARE DISPUTED. PLAINTIFF TRACY YU HAS AGREED TO SETTLE FOR $5,001 WITH THE DRIVER (MARINI) AND OWNER (POPPEL) OF ONE OF THE OTHER TWO VEHICLES. THE THIRD VEHICLE'S DRIVER, PETER GIORDANO, OPPOSES A GOOD FAITH DETERMINATION WITH THREE POINTS. FIRST, HE SAYS HIS VEHICLE WAS PUSHED INTO THE YU VEHICLE BY THE MARINI/POPPEL VEHICLE, SO HE HAS LIMITED LIABILITY COMPARED TO THEM. (OPP. 1:25-2:3.) SECOND, GIORDANO REPORTS THAT, TWO WEEKS AFTER AGREEING TO SETTLE WITH MARINI AND POPPEL FOR $5,001, YU ESTIMATED HER DAMAGES AT $472,108.50 AND DEMANDED $300,000 FROM GIORDANO. (ID. AT 2:8-14.) THIRD, GIORDANO ALSO REPORTS THAT MARINI AND POPPEL ARE COVERED BY INSURANCE WITH LIMITS OF $100,000 PER PERSON AND $300,000 PER INCIDENT. (ID. AT 4:4-8.) PREVAILING PARTY TO RESUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT TRACKS THE RULING OF THE COURT VERBATIM IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-16-2023 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-11-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ADRIANNA MARINI and AUDREY POPPEL'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Adriana Marini and Audrey Poppel's "motion for determination of good faith settlement" is denied.

The case involves a three-vehicle in-line accident on US 101, the facts of which are disputed. Plaintiff Tracy Yu has agreed to settle for $5,001 with the driver (Marini) and owner (Poppel) of one of the other two vehicles.

The third vehicle's driver, Peter Giordano, opposes a good faith determination with three points. First, he says his vehicle was pushed into the Yu vehicle by the Marini/Poppel vehicle, so he has limited liability compared to them. (Opp. 1:25-2:3.) Second, Giordano reports that, two weeks after agreeing to settle with Marini and Poppel for $5,001, Yu estimated her damages at $472,108.50 and demanded $300,000 from Giordano. (Id. at 2:8-14.) Third, Giordano also reports that Marini and Poppel are covered by insurance with limits of $100,000 per person and $300,000 per incident. (Id. at 4:4-8.)

Prevailing party to resubmit a proposed order that tracks the ruling of the Court verbatim in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-09-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ADRIANNA MARINI, AND AUDREY POPPEL's Motion for Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The "motion for leave to filed cross-complaint" is granted. Absent bad faith, it is "mandatory to grant such a motion." (Silver Organization Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 97.)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
